steps.title: Identify the pattern the evidence must show
steps.content: The researchers conclude that electric lighting prompted a behavioral shift—more nighttime activity. The evidence supporting this must show increased nighttime activity references in novels published after electric lighting spread in the late 1800s.

steps.title: Find which choice demonstrates more nighttime activity after lighting spread
steps.content: B shows the pattern exactly: novels after 1880 contain significantly more references to activities occurring after 10 p.m., revealing the behavioral change that electric lighting enabled. A, C, and D either address daytime hours (irrelevant to lighting), track reference types rather than actual behavior, or show no temporal shift. This matches choice B — B is correct.
